jokes.text: Sir, I hate to disturb you during feeding time...
jokes.setup: Geoffrey needs to report something urgent to Uncle Phil
jokes.punchline: Refers to Uncle Phil's eating as 'feeding time' like he's an animal
jokes.explanation: Classic Geoffrey snark - perfectly in character for the sarcastic butler to dehumanize Uncle Phil's eating habits

jokes.text: Oh, my God, Uncle Phil, he's got... a business card.
jokes.setup: Will appears terrified about what the intruder is holding
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's just an innocuous business card
jokes.explanation: Well-structured misdirection joke with good timing and delivery setup

jokes.text: How much? / Shut up, Carlton.
jokes.setup: Carlton eagerly asks about money
jokes.punchline: Uncle Phil immediately shuts him down
jokes.explanation: Sets up Carlton's money obsession running gag - perfectly in character

jokes.text: How much? / Shut up, Carlton.
jokes.setup: Carlton immediately asks about money again
jokes.punchline: Uncle Phil repeats the exact same shutdown
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback execution - the repetition makes it funnier and establishes the dynamic

jokes.text: I'm going to pack my stuff, all right?
jokes.setup: Half million dollar offer is announced
jokes.punchline: Will immediately abandons all sentimentality and prepares to move
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Will's pragmatism wins over sentiment instantly

jokes.text: Let's hope so. / Very funny, Carlton.
jokes.setup: Someone says money isn't always the bottom line
jokes.punchline: Carlton genuinely hopes he's the only one who thinks that way
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton - his materialistic values are so ingrained he can't hide them

jokes.text: My father would kill me if he knew I was out with a boy tonight. I told him I was going to church. / The Lord is good.
jokes.setup: Ashley mentions lying to her father about going to church
jokes.punchline: Will blasphemously thanks God for the deception
jokes.explanation: Clever religious irony - Will treating deception as divine blessing is perfectly irreverent

jokes.text: Well, you are pretty slick, Will Smith. / And, I ain't even oiled up yet, you know.
jokes.setup: Ashley calls Will 'slick' as a compliment
jokes.punchline: Will takes it literally, suggesting he could be even more slippery with oil
jokes.explanation: Classic Will - turns a metaphor literal with sexual innuendo in typical smooth-talking style

jokes.text: Hey, you know what? You can sell the house. It's the couch that got the memories.
jokes.setup: Family discusses sentimental attachment to the house
jokes.punchline: Will reduces all memories to just the couch where he hooks up
jokes.explanation: Great character insight - Will's priorities are hilariously different from the family's

jokes.text: Out where? / To the trash cans. You know, those things you hit every time you back out the car. / Thank God, I thought it was the neighbor's kids.
jokes.setup: Vivian explains Hilary hits the trash cans when backing out
jokes.punchline: Hilary is relieved she's only been hitting trash cans, not children
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy - Hilary's casual relief about potentially killing children is perfectly horrifying and in-character

jokes.text: This is gross. I don't touch greasy, disgusting things. / Well, just pretend it's buying you dinner afterwards.
jokes.setup: Hilary refuses to touch something gross
jokes.punchline: Will implies she regularly touches gross things (men) for money/dinner
jokes.explanation: Sharp roast - Will turns Hilary's princess attitude into a prostitution joke

jokes.text: And for your information, dinner comes first.
jokes.setup: Will makes prostitution implication
jokes.punchline: Hilary corrects the order of events, missing the insult entirely
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Hilary's obliviousness makes her accidentally confirm Will's insult

jokes.text: It was amazing and endless and magnificent. I think I saw God. / Don't stop.
jokes.setup: Carlton describes something in orgasmic terms
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey encourages him to continue, creating awkward sexual tension
jokes.explanation: Solid cringe comedy - the sexual language creates uncomfortable implications

jokes.text: You'd think in a house this big, a fellow would get a little privacy. / Or stronger doors.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil complains about lack of privacy
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey suggests the doors should be stronger to contain Uncle Phil
jokes.explanation: Classic Geoffrey snark - implies Uncle Phil breaks doors with his weight

jokes.text: If we end up moving into another guesthouse... I'm getting the bedroom this time.
jokes.setup: Family might move
jokes.punchline: Carlton assumes they'll be poor enough to live in a guesthouse again
jokes.explanation: References the show's premise - Carlton's privileged perspective on their 'poor' living situation

jokes.text: Don't worry, we'll bring a shoebox you can sleep in.
jokes.setup: Carlton wants the bedroom
jokes.punchline: Will offers him a shoebox instead, playing on his small size
jokes.explanation: Standard height joke - economical but predictable Carlton roast

jokes.text: I can. How about sharing a jail cell with you?
jokes.setup: Will says he can't imagine anything more irritating than rooming together
jokes.punchline: Carlton escalates to jail cell - implying criminal future for Will
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and character work - Carlton's comeback plays on racial stereotypes he believes

jokes.text: Well, isn't that the munchkin calling the midget short.
jokes.setup: Carlton calls Will immature
jokes.punchline: Will twists 'pot calling kettle black' into height-based insults for both of them
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay - takes familiar phrase and makes it about their shared shortness

jokes.text: What are you supposed to be? / My idol, Macaulay Culkin. / And you know why he's my idol? / Why? Because y'all the same height?
jokes.setup: Carlton reveals Macaulay Culkin is his idol and asks if Will knows why
jokes.punchline: Will assumes it's because they're both short
jokes.explanation: Well-structured setup and payoff - Will turns Carlton's earnest moment into height joke

jokes.text: And to think, I laid out 50 big ones on your damn cake. And not just any cake, I got you that really neat one in the commercials.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil is angry about spending money on cake
jokes.punchline: Describes expensive cake like a child would - 'really neat one in the commercials'
jokes.explanation: Character humor - Uncle Phil's childlike enthusiasm for TV advertised cake

jokes.text: Well, how about I'll take 'always embarrassing me' for 500, Alex.
jokes.setup: Carlton asks what he does to embarrass Will
jokes.punchline: Will references Jeopardy game show format to list Carlton's flaws
jokes.explanation: Clever pop culture reference - uses Jeopardy format to roast Carlton creatively

jokes.text: You're proud of me? You never said that to me before in your life. / Give me a hug. / You see, man, that is exactly why I'll never tell you nothing.
jokes.setup: Carlton gets emotional about Will being proud of him
jokes.punchline: Will immediately regrets showing affection when Carlton gets clingy
jokes.explanation: Perfect character dynamics - Will's masculine reluctance vs Carlton's emotional neediness

jokes.text: We got some baby Tylenol in the kitchen.
jokes.setup: Carlton has a migraine
jokes.punchline: Will offers baby medicine, referencing Carlton's size and maturity
jokes.explanation: Solid Carlton roast - combines size joke with maturity dig efficiently

jokes.text: Yes, there is a God! / Look at you all. Will say something. / Ching, ching!
jokes.setup: Family learns of million dollar offer
jokes.punchline: Carlton celebrates religiously while Will makes cash register sounds
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - Carlton's fake spirituality vs Will's honest greed

jokes.text: Ching, ching!
jokes.setup: Family asked to respond to million dollar offer
jokes.punchline: Will makes cash register sound
jokes.explanation: Simple sound effect humor, gets the point across but not creative

jokes.text: Now, listen, Uncle Phil could put that money in the bank, right... and then start getting all kinds of crazy interest. Then it's bonds, and then there's that T-bill stuff... and then they got some certificates, and then junk be compounded. / What are you talking about? / I have no idea.
jokes.setup: Will tries to explain complex financial concepts to Ashley
jokes.punchline: Admits he has no idea what he's talking about
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-aware humor - Will's confident delivery followed by honest admission of ignorance

jokes.text: A lot more than you, Carlton.
jokes.setup: Carlton questions how much excitement Ashley could have had
jokes.punchline: Ashley suggests Carlton has had even less excitement
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective - Ashley turns Carlton's condescension back on him

jokes.text: Can I blow in your ear? / Why? / I don't know, I heard Will say it.
jokes.setup: Young Robert asks to blow in Ashley's ear romantically
jokes.punchline: Admits he doesn't know why, just copying Will
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - innocent kid copying adult behavior he doesn't understand

jokes.text: Hey, Ashley, you ain't telling me that dude blew in your ear. / Me either. / I embellished.
jokes.setup: Will and Carlton are shocked about ear-blowing
jokes.punchline: Ashley casually admits she made it up
jokes.explanation: Nice misdirection - builds up the scandal then deflates it with Ashley's honesty

jokes.text: This is a stick with a snake wrapped around it. / It's a symbol, like Prince. / Prince who?
jokes.setup: Kid explains his symbolic name like Prince the musician
jokes.punchline: Uncle Phil doesn't know who Prince is
jokes.explanation: Good generational humor - Uncle Phil's cultural disconnect from youth

jokes.text: See, that's exactly why fathers ain't allowed at the mall.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil doesn't understand youth culture
jokes.punchline: Will explains this is why fathers are banned from teen spaces
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor - turns specific moment into universal truth about parent-teen dynamics

jokes.text: It's a symbol for 'get out of my house!'
jokes.setup: Discussion about symbolic names
jokes.punchline: Uncle Phil creates his own symbol meaning get out
jokes.explanation: Good callback to symbol concept with Uncle Phil's protective father energy

jokes.text: I think you must have her confused with your mama.
jokes.setup: Someone describes Ashley with insulting physical characteristics
jokes.punchline: Will turns the insult back with classic 'your mama' comeback
jokes.explanation: Classic street comeback - predictable but perfectly delivered and in-character for Will

jokes.text: He says it's time for me to leave the nest, become independent... and have sex with girls. / Safe sex, mind you, but lots of it.
jokes.setup: Carlton explains Will's life advice
jokes.punchline: Emphasizes the sexual aspect in his innocent way
jokes.explanation: Great cringe - Carlton's earnest delivery of sexual advice creates uncomfortable comedy

jokes.text: That life is one big orgy. / I ain't never said... / College is hard work, son... followed by a family, which is even more hard work. And a teenage daughter who doesn't wanna be seen with you in public. And a wife who won't let you anywhere near her. And a butler who may be the father of your child.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil accuses Will of promoting hedonism
jokes.punchline: Launches into dark rant about his own depressing life
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating rant - Uncle Phil's life complaints get increasingly dark and personal, culminating in butler paternity joke

jokes.text: You big-eared freeloader! You take your little square-headed cousin here... and you find Geoffrey, and you bring him back... or they'll never find your bodies. And I'm a judge, I can make it happen.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil is angry about something
jokes.punchline: Threatens murder and uses his judicial position to cover it up
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy - Uncle Phil's judicial corruption threat is both menacing and absurd

jokes.text: All right. All right, so I have an ugly side. / And you could write 'Goodyear' on it.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil admits he has an ugly side
jokes.punchline: Will says it's big enough to write Goodyear on like a tire
jokes.explanation: Brilliant fat joke - creative tire metaphor with perfect economy and timing

jokes.text: I ain't no bungee expert or nothing... but I don't think he's supposed to be slamming into the ground like that.
jokes.setup: Someone is bungee jumping badly
jokes.punchline: Will's understated observation about obvious death/injury
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy - Will's casual expertise disclaimer before stating the obvious about a death

jokes.text: If I hadn't asked for a special proposal... Trevor would be in my arms... instead of in my purse.
jokes.setup: Hilary reflects on her boyfriend's bungee death
jokes.punchline: Reveals she's carrying his remains in her purse
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - the casual way Hilary mentions carrying cremated remains creates perfect morbid humor

jokes.text: Hilary... Trevor wasn't cremated. / Then who's this?
jokes.setup: Geoffrey reveals Trevor wasn't cremated
jokes.punchline: Hilary realizes she's been carrying random ashes
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy escalation - makes the previous joke even more absurd and morbid

jokes.text: Whatever he hasn't eaten.
jokes.setup: Asked what food they have in the house
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey implies Uncle Phil has eaten everything
jokes.explanation: Classic Geoffrey - economical fat joke delivered with perfect butler disdain

jokes.text: Geoffrey, go fetch my tools. / You mean your knife and fork?
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil asks for his tools
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey suggests his eating utensils are his tools
jokes.explanation: Another solid Geoffrey fat joke - implies Uncle Phil's only skill is eating

jokes.text: Five months this Tuesday.
jokes.setup: Discussion about marital intimacy
jokes.punchline: Vivian precisely tracks how long it's been
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - the specificity makes the dry spell more uncomfortable

jokes.text: I weigh the same thing I weighed in high school. / Yeah, if you add up all four years.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil claims he weighs the same as in high school
jokes.punchline: Will says only if you add all four years together
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup and payoff - mathematically sound fat joke with perfect structure

jokes.text: It's The Donald! Oh, my God!
jokes.setup: Donald Trump appears
jokes.punchline: Carlton's over-the-top celebrity worship reaction
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate reaction - Carlton's materialistic worship of wealth symbols

jokes.text: You know, you look much richer in person.
jokes.setup: Hilary meets Donald Trump
jokes.punchline: Comments on his wealth as a physical appearance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary - treats wealth like a physical attribute, perfectly shallow

jokes.text: Tell you what, throw in another 50 grand, I'll cut the grass for you every Saturday.
jokes.setup: Will talks to Trump about the house deal
jokes.punchline: Offers lawn service for huge money
jokes.explanation: Good Will opportunism - tries to profit from the situation in typical style

jokes.text: And all this time I thought our address was 805 Saint Cloud Road. / It is, Hilary.
jokes.setup: Realtor says buyer wanted 508 Saint Cloud Road
jokes.punchline: Hilary thinks their address is wrong, but it's correct
jokes.explanation: Classic Hilary confusion - doesn't understand the address mix-up

jokes.text: It's just as well, it's too much of a fixer-upper.
jokes.setup: Deal falls through on the wrong house
jokes.punchline: Trump dismisses the mansion as a fixer-upper
jokes.explanation: Good Trump characterization - casual dismissal of luxury mansion as inadequate

jokes.text: He'll be the guy carrying the millions of dollars in cash.
jokes.setup: Someone asks where Trump went
jokes.punchline: Will describes him by the cash he's carrying
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor - reduces Trump to his most relevant feature

jokes.text: Of course, I do hear Des Moines is great this time of year.
jokes.setup: Family deciding they're happy to stay in their mansion
jokes.punchline: Will sarcastically suggests moving to unglamorous Des Moines
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic geographic humor contrasting with their Bel-Air lifestyle
